mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should deliver positive returns over the next month when policy is not tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is falling or unchanged, or if the sign predictability disappears in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% tightens liquidity and raises equity discount rates, so after the threshold is crossed the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support equity earnings expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not align with positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the relationship flips sign in a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration signals recession risk and should pressure the S&P 500 lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-threshold episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the average forward return is not negative relative to non-triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling HOUST and weakening RSAFS signals demand rollover in housing and retail activity, which should be bearish for the S&P 500 over the next quarter because earnings breadth deteriorates together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no negative edge over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly move in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and supply-side momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through stronger cyclical earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the sign of the relationship is unstable across rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, payroll deterioration confirms broad labor weakening and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration does not predict negative 5-day SPX returns better than either proxy alone, or if triggered negative-return frequency is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 after prior weakness should support risk appetite and equities, so positive quarterly GDP momentum should map to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 momentum does not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of post-shock episodes, or if the effect is absent in any expansionary subperiod.
